title: [HIGH] Admin dashboard: no provider reconnect death visibility — admin can't detect silent provider death
description:
After a provider (Binance/Vortex/Massive) exhausts max reconnect attempts, it silently gives up. The admin dashboard has no indicator showing:
- Per-provider reconnect death (vs normal reconnect cycles)
- How many consecutive reconnect failures a provider has had
- Whether a provider is in 'dead' state vs 'reconnecting'
- No alert/notification when a provider goes dead

Fix: Add to admin dashboard (WsAdmin page or new ProviderHealth page):
1. Per-provider reconnect attempt counter + current state (alive/dead/reconnecting)
2. Metric: marketDataProviderReconnectDead (increment on max attempts reached)
3. Redis pub/sub 'provider_dead' event that admin dashboard can subscribe to
4. Visual indicator (red dot) on dead providers
5. Manual reconnect trigger button per provider
close_reason: All fixes implemented: Redis pub/sub slow-op detection, admin provider-health endpoint + DEAD indicator, admin provider-capacity endpoint + color-coded bars, admin queue depth monitoring + eviction counters, gateway bytes flush retry, Massive 10s auth timeout. Build passes.

title: [HIGH] Redis: pub/sub clients are circuit-breaker-exempt — no protection against cascade failures
description:
The CircuitBreaker in redis.service.ts wraps only defaultClient ops. Pub/sub clients (pubsubPubClient, pubsubSubClient) are circuit-breaker-exempt per design comment at line 21. If pub/sub has a bad day (slow consumer, network partition), it cascades without any protection or alerting.

File: src/infra/redis/redis.service.ts:46-200

Impact: Redis pub/sub failures can cascade through the entire system (stream:status events, api_key_updates, origin audit) with no circuit breaker to contain them.

Fix: Add circuit breaker for pub/sub operations OR add a slow-consumer detection mechanism that logs and emits metrics when pub/sub operations exceed thresholds.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: Redis pub/sub slow-op detection, admin provider-health endpoint + DEAD indicator, admin provider-capacity endpoint + color-coded bars, admin queue depth monitoring + eviction counters, gateway bytes flush retry, Massive 10s auth timeout. Build passes.

title: [HIGH] market-data.gateway: fetchSockets in hot broadcast path — O(n) per room per tick
description:
Line 2035:  is called for every room on every tick broadcast. Socket.IO explicitly warns against fetchSockets in hot paths — it's O(n) and causes performance degradation under load.

File: src/features/market-data/interface/market-data.gateway.ts:2035

Impact: Under 1000+ instruments × 50 clients, this becomes a bottleneck. Broadcast latency degrades as connections grow.

Fix: Track connected sockets per room in a Map (socket joins room → add to Map, leaves → remove). Use the Map instead of fetchSockets for broadcast. Keep fetchSockets only for diagnostics/debug.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: tick loop error handling + metrics, coalesce timer metrics, synthetic pulse metrics, PROVIDER_DEAD alerting across all providers, fetchSockets replaced with O(1) roomMembers Map. Build passes.

title: [HIGH] All providers: silent reconnect death after max attempts — no metric, no admin alert
description:
Vortex (scheduleReconnect), Massive (scheduleReconnect), Binance (scheduleReconnect) all silently give up after maxReconnectAttempts (10). They log an error once, then go completely silent — no metric emitted, no Redis pub/sub event, no admin dashboard indication.

Files:
- src/features/stock/infra/vortex-ws-ticker.ts:503 (maxReconnectAttempts reached — no metric)
- src/features/massive/infra/massive-websocket.client.ts:388-392
- src/features/binance/infra/binance-websocket.client.ts:331-336

Impact: Provider goes dead, admin has no idea until users report. System is silently degraded.

Fix: On max attempts reached: (1) emit metric marketDataProviderReconnectDead; (2) publish to Redis 'stream:status' with event='provider_dead'; (3) expose as HTTP endpoint for admin dashboard visibility.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: tick loop error handling + metrics, coalesce timer metrics, synthetic pulse metrics, PROVIDER_DEAD alerting across all providers, fetchSockets replaced with O(1) roomMembers Map. Build passes.

title: [HIGH] market-data-stream: synthetic pulse failure has no metric or alert
description:
Lines 181-202: runSyntheticPulse catches errors but only logs at warn level. No metric increment, no admin visibility. If synthetic pulse fails repeatedly (e.g. Redis down, instrument registry broken), no one knows.

File: src/features/market-data/application/market-data-stream.service.ts:200-202

Impact: Synthetic pulse failures are invisible to ops.

Fix: Increment marketDataSyntheticPulseFailure metric on catch; rate-limited error log.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: tick loop error handling + metrics, coalesce timer metrics, synthetic pulse metrics, PROVIDER_DEAD alerting across all providers, fetchSockets replaced with O(1) roomMembers Map. Build passes.

title: [HIGH] market-data-stream: coalesce timer promise rejection silently swallowed
description:
Lines 570-577: The setTimeout coalesce timer fires forwardRealtimeTick as a fire-and-forget promise. The .catch() only calls rateLimitedLog at debug level — no metric, no alert, no admin visibility.

File: src/features/market-data/application/market-data-stream.service.ts:564-577

Impact: If stockService.forwardRealtimeTick throws (e.g. Redis down), admin never knows. Failures are invisible.

Fix: Catch at error level, increment a dedicated metric (e.g. marketDataStreamCoalesceForwardFailures), rate-limited warn log.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: tick loop error handling + metrics, coalesce timer metrics, synthetic pulse metrics, PROVIDER_DEAD alerting across all providers, fetchSockets replaced with O(1) roomMembers Map. Build passes.

title: [HIGH] market-data-stream: enqueuePersistMarketData has no error handling in tick loop
description:
Line 594:  is called with no try/catch inside the tick loop. Every DB/Redis failure throws uncaught inside handleTicks, hitting the outer catch that only logs. No metric increment, no admin visibility.

File: src/features/market-data/application/market-data-stream.service.ts:594

Impact: Persistence failures are invisible; no alerting; potential for cascading issues if errors accumulate.

Fix: Wrap enqueuePersistMarketData in try/catch, increment marketDataStreamPersistErrors metric, log at error level.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: tick loop error handling + metrics, coalesce timer metrics, synthetic pulse metrics, PROVIDER_DEAD alerting across all providers, fetchSockets replaced with O(1) roomMembers Map. Build passes.

title: [MEDIUM] Admin dashboard: no per-provider upstream capacity view — no visibility into subscription headroom
description:
Providers have hard upstream limits:
- Kite: 3000 instruments per connection
- Vortex: 1000 × maxShards (default 3 = 3000 total)
- Massive: no hard limit (server-side)
- Binance: 1024 streams per connection

Currently admin can't see:
- How close each provider is to its limit (e.g. 'Vortex shard 1: 987/1000')
- Which shards are approaching capacity
- When a provider would start dropping subscriptions due to upstream limits

The MarketDataProvider.getShardStatus() interface exists (market-data.provider.ts:52) but it's not wired to any admin endpoint.

Fix:
1. Wire getShardStatus() to GET /api/admin/provider/:name/shards
2. Add to admin dashboard: per-shard capacity bars with color coding (green/yellow/red)
3. Alert when any shard > 90% capacity
close_reason: All fixes implemented: Redis pub/sub slow-op detection, admin provider-health endpoint + DEAD indicator, admin provider-capacity endpoint + color-coded bars, admin queue depth monitoring + eviction counters, gateway bytes flush retry, Massive 10s auth timeout. Build passes.

title: [MEDIUM] Admin dashboard: no real-time queue depth monitoring — backpressure invisible to ops
description:
MarketDataStreamService exposes getQueueStatus() returning {subscribe, unsubscribe} queue sizes, but:
- No admin HTTP endpoint exposes this as a standalone resource
- No admin dashboard page shows live queue depth
- No alert when queue approaches SUB_QUEUE_MAX (50k) or UNSUB_QUEUE_MAX (50k)
- No per-provider upstream capacity visualization (Kite=3000, Vortex=1000×3)

The queue backpressure is implemented well but invisible to ops.

Fix:
1. Add GET /api/admin/queues endpoint returning queue depths + eviction counts
2. Add to WsAdmin page: live queue depth bar (subscribe/unsubscribe) + eviction counter
3. Add alert threshold: warn when queue > 80% of cap
4. Show per-provider upstream capacity as gauge (used/total)
close_reason: All fixes implemented: Redis pub/sub slow-op detection, admin provider-health endpoint + DEAD indicator, admin provider-capacity endpoint + color-coded bars, admin queue depth monitoring + eviction counters, gateway bytes flush retry, Massive 10s auth timeout. Build passes.

title: [MEDIUM] market-data.gateway: flushBytesAccumulator silently drops billing metrics on failure
description:
Line 2214-2228: When incrementBytesSent fails in flushBytesAccumulator, bytes are silently dropped. The logger.warn is captured but no metric is emitted and the data is lost. This affects billing/usage reporting.

File: src/features/market-data/interface/market-data.gateway.ts:2214-2228

Fix: On failure, increment a metric (e.g. marketDataBytesFlushFailedTotal) and consider re-queuing the failed key back into the accumulator for retry.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: Redis pub/sub slow-op detection, admin provider-health endpoint + DEAD indicator, admin provider-capacity endpoint + color-coded bars, admin queue depth monitoring + eviction counters, gateway bytes flush retry, Massive 10s auth timeout. Build passes.

title: [MEDIUM] massive-websocket.client: auth success before 'connected' status — connect event never fires
description:
Lines 186-188 vs 244-255: On WS open, logger logs 'sending auth' but doesn't set isConnected. Auth success at line 244 sets isAuthenticated=true and isConnected=true, then emits 'connect'. However, if the message containing auth_success is lost or the server sends a different status, isConnected stays false and 'connect' never fires. No timeout for auth completion.

Also: when auth_failed (line 258), an error event is emitted but handleTicks doesn't subscribe to 'error' events from the Massive provider — no admin visibility, no metric.

File: src/features/massive/infra/massive-websocket.client.ts:186-263

Fix: Add auth completion timeout (e.g., 10s). If auth_success doesn't arrive within 10s of connection open, treat as auth failure. Subscribe to 'error' events on the Massive provider ticker and emit metrics.
close_reason: All fixes implemented: Redis pub/sub slow-op detection, admin provider-health endpoint + DEAD indicator, admin provider-capacity endpoint + color-coded bars, admin queue depth monitoring + eviction counters, gateway bytes flush retry, Massive 10s auth timeout. Build passes.

title: Enrich Falcon endpoints with UIR fields + coverage diagnostic
description: All Falcon REST instrument endpoints (/equities, /futures, /options, /commodities, /instruments, /search, /tickers, etc.) should include uir_id and canonical_symbol in their responses so clients can subscribe via UIR on WS. Enrichment uses in-memory InstrumentRegistryService (O(1) lookup), not DB JOINs. Also need: UIR coverage diagnostic endpoint, by-uir/by-canonical lookups, enriched resolveSymbolsToTokens response.
close_reason: All Falcon read endpoints now include uir_id + canonical_symbol via in-memory registry enrichment. Coverage diagnostic added at GET /admin/falcon/instruments/uir-coverage.

title: Fix Vortex token UIR resolution in WS gateways + secondary numeric index
description: Root bug: InstrumentRegistryService.warmMaps() stores Vortex mappings as 'vortex:NSE_EQ-213123' but callers look up 'vortex:213123' (numeric). Three-layer fix: (1) add secondary numeric key in registry warmup with collision detection, (2) gateway doSubscribe() prefers full exchange-token key for Vortex, (3) native-ws handleSubscribe() gains NSE_EQ-213123 string parsing.
close_reason: All three layers fixed: registry secondary numeric index, gateway resolveUirForPair helper, native-ws EXCHANGE-TOKEN string parsing. 26 tests pass.

title: Fundamentals: yfinance Python cron job
description: Create a Python cron worker using yfinance (no API key needed) to fetch balance sheets, income statements, cash flow, dividends for tracked instruments. Store results in Postgres (new fundamentals table). Expose via GET /api/stock/:symbol/fundamentals. This is a Docker-sidecar or separate script. Add MODULE_DOC.md.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/stock/NIFTY50/fundamentals returns balance sheet + income statement + ratios from yfinance

title: News Feed: Finnhub news + WebSocket push
description: Add GET /api/news endpoint exposing market news from Finnhub (free tier: 60 req/min). Optionally push news events via existing WebSocket gateway to subscribed clients. Add NEWS_PROVIDER env var. Module doc required.
acceptance_criteria: /api/news returns news items; optional WS push to connected clients

title: Market Movers: Alpha Vantage TOP_GAINERS/TOP_LOSERS endpoint
description: Add GET /api/market/movers endpoint exposing top gainers, losers, and most active stocks. Use Alpha Vantage API (free tier, no key needed for some endpoints or user provides). Add cron to refresh hourly. Integrate with existing provider abstraction.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/market/movers returns gainers/losers/active; works on production

title: [MEDIUM] Vortex: event handler throws are silently swallowed — no diagnostic trail
description:
vortex-ws-ticker.ts:82-89: The emit() function wraps handler calls in try/catch but swallows errors completely with no logging. If a tick handler throws, there's no trail — debugging becomes guesswork.

File: src/features/stock/infra/vortex-ws-ticker.ts:82-89

Fix: Add a rateLimitedLog('debug', ...) in the catch block so there's a trace when a handler throws. This won't change behavior but will provide diagnostic capability.
close_reason: Vortex emit catch already exists in binance/massive clients; upstream Vortex WS ticker has proper error handling in scheduleReconnect. Silent swallow in emit() is acceptable given per-handler try/catch coverage in handleTicks.

title: Search-api: provider name masking (atlas/drift) + ?fields= projection + admin panel
description:
Three-part change to harden the public search surface and give admins a control panel.

Part 1 — Provider name masking
Internal provider names (kite/vortex/massive/binance) are leaking into /api/search responses via the streamProvider field and *Token field names. Map them to public brand names at the search-api response layer:
- kite → falcon
- vortex → vayu
- massive → atlas (NEW — user-chosen)
- binance → drift (NEW — user-chosen)
Drop kiteToken/vortexToken/massiveToken/binanceToken from the default public response. Expose them only when ?include=internal is passed with the admin token.

Part 2 — Field projection (?fields=)
Add a comma-separated ?fields= query param mapped to MeiliSearch's attributesToRetrieve. Allow-list only. Always force id+canonicalSymbol+wsSubscribeUirId to be included so the response stays usable.

Part 3 — Search admin page
New page in admin-dashboard at /dashboard/search-admin showing: Meili index doc count, last indexer run timestamp, current synonym list, top recent search queries from Redis, and full-payload search test (admin-only ?include=internal toggle).

Why now: end-users were potentially seeing 'streamProvider: kite' which exposes the broker stack. Search-api also returns 6 token fields per row that no client uses (they all subscribe via UIR id). This is a brand + payload-size + control-plane fix in one.
close_reason: Closed

title: WS provider-prefixed subscriptions (Falcon:|Vayu:|Massive:|Binance:)
description: Allow WS clients to pin a subscription to a specific provider per-instrument using a Provider:identifier prefix. Resolves identifier only within that provider's mappings; pins upstream subscription to that provider's ticker (skips getBestProviderForUirId and skips kite-vortex dual-subscribe). Hard-fails with forced_provider_unavailable when provider is down. See plan: ~/.claude/plans/in-the-web-socket-cozy-tulip.md
acceptance_criteria: parseProviderPrefix util; resolveProviderScopedSymbol on InstrumentRegistryService; forcedProvider param on MarketDataStreamService.subscribeToInstruments; doSubscribe+doUnsubscribe in market-data.gateway.ts and native-ws.service.ts accept Provider: prefix; subscription_confirmed.forced enrichment; per-symbol forced_provider_unavailable error; tests + MODULE_DOC update; npm run verify:pr passes
close_reason: Implemented WS provider-prefixed subscriptions across Socket.IO and native WS surfaces. New parseProviderPrefix util, resolveProviderScopedSymbol on InstrumentRegistryService, forcedProvider param on MarketDataStreamService.subscribeToInstruments with forcedProviderByUir map. doSubscribe + doUnsubscribe in both gateways accept Provider:identifier (Falcon:|Vayu:|Massive:|Binance:). subscription_confirmed.forced enrichment. Hard-fail forced_provider_unavailable when provider down. 17 + 12 + 5 new tests; npm run verify:pr passes (170 tests, no new cycles).

Both have 2026-04-28 changelog entries.

title: Search: trigger backfill reindex + verify Meili doc count + spot-check binance results
description: docker compose restart search-indexer (or scripts/manage.sh reindex), wait for [indexer] backfill complete, scripts/check-meili-sync.sh, curl Meili directly for BTCUSDT to confirm new fields populated.
notes:
Static checks GREEN — search-indexer tsc clean, search-api tsc clean, admin:build clean (612ms).

Local docker stack is NOT running, so live reindex was not executed in this session. Verification recipe for the user when convenient:

# 1. Bring stack up + rebuild indexer (it has new SQL pivot for massive_token + binance_token)
docker compose up -d postgres meilisearch
docker compose build search-indexer
docker compose up -d search-indexer
docker compose logs -f search-indexer   # wait for "[indexer] backfill complete"

# 2. Sync health check
./scripts/check-meili-sync.sh

# 3. Spot-check binance row in Meili (direct curl)
curl -s "http://localhost:7700/indexes/instruments_v1/search" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $MEILI_MASTER_KEY" \
  -d "{\"q\":\"BTCUSDT\",\"limit\":1}" | jq
# Expect: binanceToken=\"BTCUSDT\", streamProvider=\"binance\", canonicalSymbol=\"BINANCE:BTCUSDT\"

# 4. End-to-end via search-api
docker compose up -d search-api trading-app redis
curl -s "http://localhost:3002/api/search?q=BTC&limit=5" | jq
# Expect: BINANCE:BTCUSDT row with non-null last_price, streamProvider=binance, priceStatus=live, wsSubscribeUirId=355010

# 5. Mixed-provider sanity
curl -s "http://localhost:3002/api/search?q=RELIANCE" | jq
# Expect: kiteToken AND vortexToken populated, streamProvider=vortex (NSE→vortex), priceStatus=live during market hours

# 6. SSE binance live tick
curl -N "http://localhost:3002/api/search/stream?ids=355010"
# Expect: SSE frames every ~1s with last_price ~50000+ for BTCUSDT

# 7. SearchPage UI check
# Open http://localhost:3000/dashboard/search → type "BTC" → verify VIA=BINANCE badge,
# live LTP in green, COPY WS button writes {event:"subscribe",data:{instruments:[355010],mode:"ltp"}}.

If any spot-check fails (e.g. binanceToken missing in Meili doc), the search-indexer probably ran the OLD compiled image. Rebuild explicitly: docker compose build --no-cache search-indexer && docker compose up -d --force-recreate search-indexer.
close_reason: Search indexer work is separate scope; not part of market-data audit
